original_hypothesis.title: ETH Pool Creation Reverts on Strict WETH Implementations
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: `Router.createPoolETH` calls `weth.safeTransferFrom(address(this), clone, msg.value)` without first approving the Router to spend its own WETH. While standard WETH9 bypasses the allowance check when `src == msg.sender`, stricter WETH implementations or L2 native equivalents might enforce the allowance check universally, causing ETH pool creation to always revert.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Relying on non-standard ERC20 behavior (allowance bypass for self-transfers) creates cross-chain deployment fragility.
deployment_assumptions: Deployed on a chain where the WETH equivalent strictly enforces allowances for self-transfers
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: Blast.s.sol uses Blast's native WETH equivalent.
preconditions: WETH implementation requires allowance even when `src == msg.sender`

operational_questions: Does Blast's WETH implementation exactly mirror WETH9's allowance bypass?
Are there plans to deploy on chains with strict WETH?

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Transaction reverts due to insufficient allowance

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Inability to create ETH pools via Router

validation_result.impact_summary: Router.createPoolETH uses safeTransferFrom to transfer the Router's own WETH tokens instead of safeTransfer, relying on WETH9's non-standard allowance bypass for self-transfers. This will revert on strict ERC20/WETH implementations.
validation_result.detailed_analysis:
The createPoolETH function at Router.sol:84 calls `address(weth).safeTransferFrom(address(this), clone, msg.value)` to transfer WETH that the Router just deposited. This is a transferFrom where both `from` and `msg.sender` are the Router contract itself. In standard WETH9, the `transferFrom` function has a special case: `if (src != msg.sender && allowance[src][msg.sender] != type(uint).max)` which skips the allowance check when src == msg.sender. However, this is a non-standard ERC20 behavior. A strict ERC20 implementation would check `allowance[Router][Router]` which is 0, causing the call to revert.

Critically, this is inconsistent with every other ETH-handling function in the same Router contract. Functions like addLiquidityETH (line ~193), addLiquidityETHUnsafe (line ~218), sellBaseETHForTokens (line ~293), swapETHForTokens (line ~267), and sellQuoteETHForTokens (line ~318) all correctly use `safeTransfer` to move the Router's own WETH tokens. Only createPoolETH incorrectly uses `safeTransferFrom`.

This is a clear code defect: the function should use `address(weth).safeTransfer(clone, msg.value)` instead of `address(weth).safeTransferFrom(address(this), clone, msg.value)`.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: Router.sol line 84 contains `address(weth).safeTransferFrom(address(this), clone, msg.value);` while every comparable function uses `safeTransfer`. For example, addLiquidityETH uses `address(weth).safeTransfer(lp, wethAdjustedAmount);` (Router.sol ~line 193), and sellBaseETHForTokens uses `baseToken.safeTransfer(lp, msg.value);` (Router.sol ~line 295). The inconsistency confirms this is a bug, not a design choice.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: Line 84 uses safeTransferFrom(address(this), clone, msg.value) to transfer the Router's own WETH. No approval is set before this call. This relies on WETH9's non-standard behavior of skipping allowance checks when src == msg.sender. On strict WETH implementations, the allowance[Router][Router] will be 0, causing a revert.

calling_sequence: 1. User calls createPoolETH with ETH value
4. weth.deposit{value: msg.value}() wraps ETH to WETH (Router.sol:82)
5. token.safeTransferFrom(msg.sender, clone, tokenInAmount) transfers user's tokens (Router.sol:83)
6. address(weth).safeTransferFrom(address(this), clone, msg.value) - VULNERABLE: attempts transferFrom on self without approval (Router.sol:84)
7. On strict WETH: reverts with insufficient allowance
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The Router deposits ETH into WETH at line 82, receiving WETH in its own balance. At line 84, instead of using safeTransfer (which only checks balance), it uses safeTransferFrom which also checks allowance[address(this)][address(this)]. Since no approve was called, the allowance is 0. WETH9 has a special bypass for this case (src == msg.sender), but strict ERC20 implementations do not.

deployment_assumptions_checked: Deployed on a chain where the WETH equivalent strictly enforces allowances for self-transfers
config_validation.deployment_evidence: MIMSwap.s.sol shows deployment only on Blast chain (line: `if (block.chainid == ChainId.Blast)` with `revert("unsupported chain")` else). The Router is constructed with `toolkit.getAddress(block.chainid, "weth")`. Blast's native WETH behavior for self-transfers is not documented in the deployment scripts. The code defect (using transferFrom instead of transfer for self-owned tokens) exists regardless, but exploitability depends on the WETH implementation.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: A user calls createPoolETH on a chain where WETH strictly enforces ERC20 allowance semantics (no self-transfer bypass). The function wraps ETH to WETH, then attempts safeTransferFrom(address(this), clone, msg.value). The WETH contract checks allowance[Router][Router] which is 0, causing the transaction to revert. ETH pool creation via the Router is permanently broken. Users cannot create ETH-paired pools through the Router on such chains. The fix is trivial: change safeTransferFrom to safeTransfer.
validation_result.impact_assessment: On chains with strict WETH implementations, createPoolETH is completely non-functional. Users cannot create ETH-paired pools through the Router. This is a DoS of a core protocol function. Even on WETH9-compatible chains, the code contains a latent defect (reliance on non-standard behavior) that makes cross-chain deployment fragile. The inconsistency with all other ETH functions in Router that correctly use safeTransfer confirms this is an unintentional bug.
recommended_mitigations: Replace `address(weth).safeTransferFrom(address(this), clone, msg.value)` with `address(weth).safeTransfer(clone, msg.value)` at Router.sol line 84
This aligns with the pattern used in all other ETH functions in Router (addLiquidityETH, sellBaseETHForTokens, swapETHForTokens, etc.)
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy a mock WETH that strictly enforces allowance checks (no src==msg.sender bypass). 2. Deploy Router with this strict WETH. 3. Call createPoolETH with some ETH and a token. 4. Observe that the transaction reverts on the safeTransferFrom call. 5. Alternatively, fork Blast mainnet and verify Blast's WETH behavior for self-transfers.
uncertainties: Blast's specific WETH implementation behavior for self-transfers is not verified in the provided code
Whether the protocol plans to deploy on chains other than Blast where WETH might be stricter
additional_testing_needed: Test createPoolETH against Blast's actual WETH implementation via mainnet fork
Verify if Blast WETH has the same src==msg.sender bypass as WETH9
Test with a strict ERC20 wrapper to confirm the revert behavior
